subClaimText: Seneca operates a dual-class (or multi-class) common share structure with differential voting rights.
summary: Source pack lists S-005 and S-001 as verification sources for C-010 and describes S-005 as containing 'dual-class share, voting, and beneficial-ownership disclosures.' Anchor only — not yet a primary-source quote.

subClaimText: Founding-family / insider holders control voting power disproportionate to economic ownership.
summary: Source pack open question Q-006 explicitly asks whether the FY2025 proxy quantifies combined Class A/Class B voting power held by the founding family/insiders, signalling that the source pack itself has not yet confirmed family voting concentration.

subClaimText: Seneca runs a limited investor-relations / promotion program (no regular earnings calls, no investor day, sparse press releases).
summary: Plan section 1 lists 'lack of coverage' as one of the testable assertions; S-006 is the only source nominated for it, with confidence 'medium' and no archived snapshot of the IR site present in the repo.

subClaimText: Seneca has no, or near-zero, active sell-side analyst coverage.
summary: Source pack claim C-009 names 'consensus-estimate vendor coverage records' as the secondary input but does not specify which vendor. No vendor pull is attached.

subClaimText: Seneca has used issuer tender offers (including potential Dutch auctions) to repurchase Class A and/or Class B shares.
summary: No SC TO-I / SC 13E / SC 13E3 filings have been pulled from EDGAR for CIK 0000088948; tender-offer history therefore cannot be confirmed or refuted from materials currently in the repo.

subClaimText: Seneca has authorized and executed open-market repurchase programs.
summary: C-008 verification method identifies 10-K Item 5 / Item 7 disclosures and proxy statements as the canonical buyback record. The intended downstream artifact 'artifacts/senea_metrics.csv (buyback_history)' has not been produced.

subClaimText: Dual-class voting concentration creates governance risk (related-party transactions, weak independent-director oversight, restrictive change-of-control provisions, or entrenchment).
summary: Without the DEF 14A and 10-K Risk Factors retrieved, related-party transactions, board-independence ratios, and change-of-control entrenchment provisions cannot be assessed.

subClaimText: Management has accelerated repurchases when the public-market price moved further below tangible / FIFO book value (capital allocation is counter-cyclical to the discount).
summary: Counter-cyclical-buyback test requires a price + book-value + buyback-intensity time series, which lives in the unproduced 'artifacts/senea_metrics.csv (buyback_history)'. Source-pack open question Q-002 also leaves the maintenance-capex / FCF context unresolved.

subClaimText: Founding-family / insider holders control voting power disproportionate to economic ownership.
summary: Contrary scenario CE-1: DEF 14A may show founding-family combined Class A + Class B voting power below a control threshold (for example, < 35%), which would weaken the dual-class control narrative.

subClaimText: Management has accelerated repurchases when the discount widened.
summary: Contrary scenario CE-2: 10-K Item 5 monthly issuer-purchase tables may show repurchases concentrated near 52-week highs rather than near tangible-book / FIFO-book lows; that would falsify SC-G8.

subClaimText: Seneca has used issuer tender offers to repurchase Class A and/or Class B shares.
citation: EDGAR CIK 0000088948 (SC TO-I docket not yet pulled); https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0000088948
summary: Contrary scenario CE-3: an SC TO-I tender offer struck at a premium materially above tangible / FIFO book value would invert the family-friendly governance interpretation (insiders extracting value, not compounding for minority holders).

subClaimText: Seneca has no active sell-side analyst coverage.
summary: Contrary scenario CE-4: if S-006 shows non-trivial active sell-side coverage, SC-G4 and the C-009 ledger claim fail.

subClaimText: Dual-class voting concentration creates governance risk.
summary: Contrary scenario CE-5: DEF 14A Item 13 may disclose material related-party flows (family-owned suppliers, related leases) that flip the governance verdict from 'controlled but aligned' to 'controlled with risk.'
